title: Triple integrals
description: Given a 3D scalar field, find the triple integral between three numeric or variable bounds.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_1^2 \int_3^4 \int_{-1}^0 x + 2y + 3z \, dz \, dy \, dx =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_1^2 \int_3^4 \int_{-1}^0 x + 2y + 3z \, dz \, dy \, dx \\\\&= \int_1^2 \int_3^4 \left[ xz + 2yz + \dfrac{3z^2}{2} \right]_{-1}^0 dy \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \int_3^4 x + 2y - \dfrac{3}{2} \, dy \, dx
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_1^2 \int_3^4 x + 2y - \dfrac{3}{2} \, dy \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \left[ xy + y^2 - \dfrac{3y}{2} \right]_3^4 dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \left( 4x + 16 - 6 \right) - \left( 3x + 9 - \dfrac{9}{2} \right) dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 x + \dfrac{11}{2} \, dx
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_1^2 x + \dfrac{11}{2} \, dx \\ \\
&= \left[ \dfrac{x^2}{2} + \dfrac{11x}{2} \right]_1^2 \\ \\
&= \left( 2 + 11 \right) - \left( \dfrac{1}{2} + \dfrac{11}{2} \right) \\ \\
&= 7
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_1^2 \int_3^4 \int_{-1}^0 x + 2y + 3z \, dz \, dy \, dx = 7$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_0^3 \int_{-2}^1 \int_1^2 x^2 - 2y + xz \, dy \, dz \, dx =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_0^3 \int_{-2}^1 \int_1^2 x^2 - 2y + xz \, dy \, dz \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^3 \int_{-2}^1 \left[ x^2y - y^2 + xzy\right]_1^2 dz \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^3 \int_{-2}^1 x^2 + xz - 3 \, dz \, dx
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_0^3 \int_{-2}^1 x^2 + xz - 3 \, dz \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^3 \left[ x^2z + \dfrac{xz^2}{2} - 3z \right]_{-2}^1 dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^3 3x^2 - \dfrac{3x}{2} - 9 \, dx
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_0^3 3x^2 - \dfrac{3x}{2} - 9 \, dx \\ \\
&= \left[ x^3 - \dfrac{3x^2}{4} - 9x \right]_0^3 \\ \\
&= 27 - \dfrac{27}{4} - 27 \\ \\
&= -\dfrac{27}{4}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_0^3 \int_{-2}^1 \int_1^2 x^2 - 2y + xz \, dy \, dz \, dx = \dfrac{-27}{4}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{-3}^1 \int_1^2 \int_0^4 y^2 - xz \, dy \, dx \, dz =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-3}^1 \int_1^2 \int_0^4 y^2 - xz \, dy \, dx \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_{-3}^1 \int_1^2 \left[ \dfrac{y^3}{3} - xzy \right]_0^4 dx \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_{-3}^1 \int_1^2 \dfrac{64}{3} - 4xz \, dx \, dz
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-3}^1 \int_1^2 \dfrac{64}{3} - 4xz \, dx \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_{-3}^1 \left[ \dfrac{64x}{3} - 2x^2z \right]_1^2 dz \\ \\
&= \int_{-3}^1 \left( \dfrac{128}{3} - 8z \right) - \left( \dfrac{64}{3} - 2z \right) dz \\ \\
&= \int_{-3}^1 \dfrac{64}{3} - 6z \, dz
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-3}^1 \dfrac{64}{3} - 6z \, dz \\ \\
&= \left[ \dfrac{64z}{3} - 3z^2 \right]_{-3}^1 \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{64}{3} - 3 \right) - \left( -64 - 27 \right) \\ \\
&= \dfrac{64}{3} + 88 \\ \\
&= \dfrac{328}{3}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_{-3}^1 \int_1^2 \int_0^4 y^2 - xz \, dy \, dx \, dz = \dfrac{328}{3}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_2^4 \int_0^1 \int_{-2}^1 z^2 + x \, dx \, dy \, dz =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_2^4 \int_0^1 \int_{-2}^1 z^2 + x \, dx \, dy \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_2^4 \int_0^1 \left[ z^2x + \dfrac{x^2}{2} \right]_{-2}^1 dy \, dz \\ \\
&=\int_2^4 \int_0^1 \left( z^2 + \dfrac{1}{2} \right) - \left( -2z + 2 \right) dy \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_2^4 \int_0^1 3z^2 - \dfrac{3}{2} \, dy \, dz
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
\int_2^4 \int_0^1 3z^2 - \dfrac{3}{2} \, dy \, dz &= \int_2^4 \left[ 3z^2y - \dfrac{3}{2} \right]_0^1 dz \\ \\
&= \int_2^4 3z^2 - \dfrac{3}{2} \, dz
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
\int_2^4 3z^2 - \dfrac{3}{2} \, dz &= \left[ z^3 - \dfrac{3z}{2} \right]_2^4 \\ \\
&= (64 - 6) - (8 - 3) \\ \\
&= 53
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_2^4 \int_0^1 \int_{-2}^1 z^2 + x \, dx \, dy \, dz = 53$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_0^2 \int_x^{2x} \int_{-1}^1 2y - 2z \, dy \, dz \, dx =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_0^2 \int_x^{2x} \int_{-1}^1 2y - 2z \, dy \, dz \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^2 \int_x^{2x} \left[ y^2 - 2yz \right]_{-1}^1 dz \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^2 \int_x^{2x} (1 - 2z) - (1 + 2z) \, dz \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^2 \int_x^{2x} -4z \, dz \, dx
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_0^2 \int_x^{2x} -4z \, dz \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^2 \left[ -2z^2 \right]_x^{2x} dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^2 -8x^2 - (-2x^2) \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_0^2 -6x^2 \, dx
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
\int_0^2 -6x^2 \, dx &= \left[ -2x^3 \right]_0^2 \\ \\
&= -16
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_0^2 \int_x^{2x} \int_{-1}^1 2y - 2z \, dy \, dz \, dx = -16$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{-1}^1 \int_0^2 \int_0^{z^2} x + 2y - 2z \, dx \, dz \, dy =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-1}^1 \int_0^2 \int_0^{z^2} x + 2y - 2z \, dx \, dz \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^1 \int_0^2 \left[ \dfrac{x^2}{2} + 2yx - 2zx \right]_0^{z^2} dz \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^1 \int_0^2 \dfrac{z^4}{2} + 2yz^2 - 2z^3 \, dz \, dy
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-1}^1 \int_0^2 \dfrac{z^4}{2} + 2yz^2 - 2z^3 \, dz \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^1 \left[ \dfrac{z^5}{10} + \dfrac{2yz^3}{3} - \dfrac{z^4}{2} \right]_0^2 dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^1 \dfrac{16}{5} + \dfrac{16y}{3} - 8 \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^1 \dfrac{16y}{3} - \dfrac{24}{5} \, dy
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-1}^1 \dfrac{16y}{3} - \dfrac{24}{5} \, dy \\ \\
&= \left[ \dfrac{8y^2}{3} - \dfrac{24y}{5} \right]_{-1}^1 \\ \\
&= \left(\dfrac{8}{3} - \dfrac{24}{5} \right) - \left( \dfrac{8}{3} + \dfrac{24}{5} \right) \\ \\
&= \dfrac{-48}{5}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_{-1}^1 \int_0^2 \int_0^{z^2} x + 2y - 2z \, dx \, dz \, dy = \dfrac{-48}{5}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_0^3 \int_0^y \int_{-1}^2 2x + 2z \, dx \, dz \, dy =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_0^3 \int_0^y \int_{-1}^2 2x + 2z \, dx \, dz \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_0^3 \int_0^y \left[ x^2 + 2xz \right]_{-1}^2 dz \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_0^3 \int_0^y 4 + 4z - 1 + 2z \, dz \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_0^3 \int_0^y 6z + 3 \, dz \, dy
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
\int_0^3 \int_0^y 6z + 3 \, dz \, dy &= \int_0^3 \left[ 3z^2 + 3z \right]_0^y \\ \\
&= \int_0^3 3y^2 + 3y
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_0^3 \int_0^y \int_{-1}^2 2x + 2z \, dx \, dz \, dy = \dfrac{81}{2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{-2}^0 \int_1^4 \int_{-x}^0 4z - 2y \, dz \, dx \, dy =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-2}^0 \int_1^4 \int_{-x}^0 4z - 2y \, dz \, dx \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-2}^0 \int_1^4 \left[ 2z^2 - 2zy \right]_{-x}^0 dx \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-2}^0 \int_1^4 -2x^2 - 2xy \, dx \, dy
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-2}^0 \int_1^4 -2x^2 - 2xy \, dx \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-2}^0 \left[ \dfrac{-2x^3}{3} - x^2y \right]_1^4 dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-2}^0 \dfrac{-128}{3} - 16y - \left( \dfrac{-2}{3} - y \right) dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-2}^0 -15y - 42 \, dy 
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
\int_{-2}^0 -15y - 42 \, dy &= \left[ \dfrac{-15y^2}{2} - 42y \right]_{-2}^0 \\ \\
&= -\left( \dfrac{-60}{2} + 84 \right) \\ \\
&= -54
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_{-2}^0 \int_1^4 \int_{-x}^0 4z - 2y \, dz \, dx \, dy = -54$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_{-1}^1 \int_0^{y^2} \int_{-x}^x 3z^2 + x - y \, dz \, dx \, dy =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-1}^1 \int_0^{y^2} \int_{-x}^x 3z^2 + x - y \, dz \, dx \, dy \\ \\
&=\int_{-1}^1 \int_0^{y^2} \left[ z^3 + xz - yz \right]_{-x}^x dx \, dy \\ \\
&=\int_{-1}^1 \int_0^{y^2} \left( x^3 + x^2 - xy \right) - \left( -x^3 - x^2 + xy \right) dx \, dy \\ \\
&=\int_{-1}^1 \int_0^{y^2} 2x^3 + 2x^2 - 2xy \, dx \, dy
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-1}^1 \int_0^{y^2} 2x^3 + 2x^2 - 2xy \, dx \, dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^1 \left[ \dfrac{x^4}{2} + \dfrac{2x^3}{3} - x^2y \right]_0^{y^2} dy \\ \\
&= \int_{-1}^1 \dfrac{y^8}{2} + \dfrac{2y^6}{3} - y^5 \, dx
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_{-1}^1 \dfrac{y^8}{2} + \dfrac{2y^6}{3} - y^5 \, dx \\ \\
&= \left[ \dfrac{y^9}{18} + \dfrac{2y^7}{21} - \dfrac{y^6}{6} \right]_{-1}^1 \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{1}{18} + \dfrac{2}{21} - \dfrac{1}{6} \right) - \left( \dfrac{-1}{18} - \dfrac{2}{21} - \dfrac{1}{6} \right) \\ \\
&= \dfrac{19}{63}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_{-1}^1 \int_0^{y^2} \int_{-x}^x 3z^2 + x - y \, dz \, dx \, dy = \dfrac{19}{63}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_1^2 \int_1^z \int_{-y}^y 2x - z \, dx \, dy \, dz =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_1^2 \int_1^z \int_{-y}^y 2x - z \, dx \, dy \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \int_1^z \left[ x^2 - zx \right]_{-y}^y dy \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \int_1^z (y^2 - zy) - (y^2 + zy) \, dy \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \int_1^z -2zy \, dy \, dz
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_1^2 \int_1^z -2zy \, dy \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \left[ -zy^2 \right]_1^z dz \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 -z^3 + z \, dz
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_1^2 -z^3 + z \, dz \\ \\
&= \left[ \dfrac{-z^4}{4} + \dfrac{z^2}{2} \right]_1^2 \\ \\
&= (-4 + 2) - \left( \dfrac{-1}{4} + \dfrac{1}{2} \right) \\ \\
&= -2 - \dfrac{1}{4} \\ \\
&= \dfrac{-9}{4}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_1^2 \int_1^z \int_{-y}^y 2x - z \, dx \, dy \, dz = \dfrac{-9}{4}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_0^1 \int_{z^2}^1 \int_z^x z - 4y \, dy \, dx \, dz =$

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_0^1 \int_{z^2}^1 \int_z^x z - 4y \, dy \, dx \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_0^1 \int_{z^2}^1 \left[ zy - 2y^2 \right]_z^x dx \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_0^1 \int_{z^2}^1 zx - 2x^2 - (z^2 - 2z^2) \, dx \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_0^1 \int_{z^2}^1 zx - 2x^2 + z^2 \, dx \, dz
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_0^1 \int_{z^2}^1 zx - 2x^2 + z^2 \, dx \, dz \\ \\
&= \int_0^1 \left[ \dfrac{zx^2}{2} - \dfrac{2x^3}{3} + z^2x \right]_{z^2}^1 dz \\ \\
&= \int_0^1 \left( \dfrac{z}{2} - \dfrac{2}{3} + z^2 \right) - \left( \dfrac{z^5}{2} - \dfrac{2z^6}{3} + z^4 \right) dz \\ \\
&= \int_0^1 \dfrac{2z^6}{3} - \dfrac{z^5}{2} - z^4 + z^2 + \dfrac{z}{2} - \dfrac{2}{3} \, dz
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_0^1 \int_{z^2}^1 \int_z^x z - 4y \, dy \, dx \, dz = \dfrac{-19}{70}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Evaluate the triple integral.**

$\displaystyle \int_1^2 \int_x^2 \int_0^y y - z \, dz \, dy \, dx =$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
We can evaluate triple integrals by repeated integration:

$\displaystyle \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \, dy \, dz = \int_{a_0}^{a_1} \left( \int_{b_0}^{b_1} \left[ \int_{c_0}^{c_1} f(x, y, z) \, dx \right] dy \right) dz$

The first layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_1^2 \int_x^2 \int_0^y y - z \, dz \, dy \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \int_x^2 \left[ yz - \dfrac{z^2}{2} \right]_0^y dy \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \int_x^2 y^2 - \dfrac{y^2}{2} \, dy \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \int_x^2 \dfrac{y^2}{2} \, dy \, dx
\end{align}$

The second layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_1^2 \int_x^2 \dfrac{y^2}{2} \, dy \, dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \left[ \dfrac{y^3}{6} \right]_x^2 dx \\ \\
&= \int_1^2 \dfrac{4}{3} - \dfrac{x^3}{6} \, dx
\end{align}$

The third layer:

$\begin{align}
&\int_1^2 \dfrac{4}{3} - \dfrac{x^3}{6} \, dx \\ \\
&= \left[ \dfrac{4x}{3} - \dfrac{x^4}{24} \right]_1^2 \\ \\
&= \left( \dfrac{8}{3} - \dfrac{16}{24} \right) - \left( \dfrac{4}{3} - \dfrac{1}{24} \right) \\ \\
&= \dfrac{2}{3} + \dfrac{1}{24} \\ \\
&= \dfrac{17}{24}
\end{align}$

In conclusion:

$\displaystyle \int_1^2 \int_x^2 \int_0^y y - z \, dz \, dy \, dx = \dfrac{17}{24}$
